title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 21
summary: Brent oil slipped after surging almost 6% over the previous two sessions, as traders weighed continued hostilities between the US and Iran against efforts to broker a new ceasefire.

title: Turkish Airlines Exploring Acquisitions in Asia, South America
summary: Turkish Airlines is exploring more acquisition opportunities as it looks to expand its global reach.

title: Goldman Says Brent Could Top $120 If Hormuz Disruptions Persist
summary: Brent could rally to more than $120 a barrel by the fourth quarter if disruptions of the Strait of Hormuz persist, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc., although that’s not the bank’s base case.

title: Stocks Extend Rally as Chips Rebound, Oil Declines: Markets Wrap
summary: Asian stocks rose for the first time in four days as investors returned to chipmakers, driving a rebound in the sector after the recent selloff. Oil declined.

title: Japan Insurers Buy Most Super-Long Bonds in Three Years in June
summary: Japanese insurance firms bought the most super-long government bonds in three years last month, adding to signs that demand from a key buyer is stabilizing.

title: Iron Ore Drops as China Steel Losses Deepen, Hormuz Risks Rise
summary: Iron ore fell for a second day as deteriorating profit margins at Chinese steel mills and continued US-Iran hostilities clouded the demand outlook.

title: China Broadens Market Rescue With Tech Stocks Drawing Support
summary: China is stepping up efforts to stabilize its stock market, mobilizing a range of state-linked institutions to stem a tech-driven selloff. The chip-heavy STAR 50 index extended gains.

title: Gold Rises on Dip-Buying as Traders Weigh Middle East Clashes
summary: Gold rose on dip-buying as traders monitored developments in the Middle East for clues on the energy price impact on inflation.

title: Global Investors Turn Cautious on Indian Bonds
summary: The rupee is also under strain, with slowing debt inflows coinciding with renewed foreign selling in stocks.

title: SBI Funds Poised for Strong India Debut After $1 Billion IPO
summary: SBI Funds Management Ltd. is poised for a strong trading debut Tuesday after investors piled into its $1 billion initial public offering, making it one of India’s most heavily subscribed billion-dollar share sales.

title: Moody’s Flags Rising Risks in Indonesia on Policy Uncertainty
summary: Moody’s Ratings remains concerned about policy uncertainty and fiscal sustainability risks in Indonesia, reinforcing its cautious outlook and warning that downside risks are likely to persist.

title: Korean Stock Traders Slash Margin Loans to Lowest Since April
summary: South Korean investors slashed their leveraged stock positions to the lowest level in three months, as memory-chip losses halted the market’s rally.

title: Global Stocks Lift Australian Pension Funds to 9.5% Annual Gain
summary: A rally in global equities delivered another strong year for Australia’s A$4.4 trillion ($3.1 trillion) pension fund industry, despite lingering concerns over the Middle East conflict and stubborn inflation.

title: Australia’s Stock Market Emerges as Haven From Volatile AI Trade
summary: Australia’s stock market has emerged as an unlikely haven from the artificial intelligence-driven volatility that has roiled Asian equities in recent months.

title: New Zealand’s Faster Inflation Bolsters Case for More Rate Hikes
summary: New Zealand’s annual inflation accelerated to the fastest pace in more than two years, fanned by soaring fuel prices and bolstering the case for the Reserve Bank to execute back-to-back interest-rate increases.

title: Kioxia Bulls Ride Out Volatility With Targets 130% Above Market
summary: Analysts are maintaining bullish views on Kioxia Holdings Inc. despite a deepening share price correction, saying the stock is likely to resume its upward trajectory once supply-demand conditions improve.

title: LA’s $1.2 Billion ‘Graffiti Towers’ Cleared to Exit Bankruptcy
summary: The bankrupt $1.2 billion Oceanwide Plaza project in Los Angeles, dubbed the Graffiti Towers after taggers spray-painted the abandoned complex, has been cleared for sale and possible completion more than seven years after the original Chinese developer ran out of money.

title: Gilts Rattled on Andy Burnham’s First Day as Fiscal Fears Return
summary: UK government bonds dropped after new Prime Minister Andy Burnham unnerved investors on his first day in the job over his approach to the country’s finances.

title: Netflix Taps US High-Grade Bond Market With $1 Billion Debt Sale
summary: Netflix Inc. raised $1 billion from a US high-grade bond sale on Monday, its first since a debut deal two years ago, as slowing sales growth fuels investor scrutiny of the company’s future.

title: Latin America Outperforms as Emerging Market Currencies Gain
summary: Emerging-market currencies gained to start the week, with Latin American names getting a boost from a drop in volatility which further boosts their carry appeal.
